channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Tarcísio pede empréstimo de R$ 1 bilhão para alça e free flow no Rodoanel
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas is seeking approval from Alesp for a R$ 1 billion loan, part of a R$ 15 billion package, to fund access ramps and the Free Flow electronic toll system on the South and East sections of the Rodoanel Mário Covas. The total project cost is R$ 1.2 billion, with R$ 355 million already sourced from the state treasury. The financing aims to compensate concessionaire SPMAR for contractual updates and includes upgrades to the Alto Tietê Complex, while the Free Flow system has drawn criticism from opposition figures who call it an 'invisible toll'.

item.title: Iván Cepeda: “Nuestro enfrentamiento no es con Paloma ni Abelardo, es con Uribe”
item.description: Iván Cepeda, a Colombian presidential candidate in the 2026 elections, states that his main political confrontation is not with his direct polling rivals, Abelardo de La Espriella and Paloma Valencia, but with Álvaro Uribe. He aims to win in the first round without triumphalism, emphasizing that his campaign is driven by an ideological struggle against the uribist legacy, despite sharing an affluent Bogotá neighborhood with his opponents.

item.title: Lula indica chance de Alckmin no Senado e pede formação de chapa forte em SP
item.description: Lula suggested that Vice President Geraldo Alckmin could run for the Senate in 2026, praising his loyalty and competence, but stressed that the decision depends on discussions with Haddad about where he could yield the most benefits. Alckmin has already indicated he prefers to remain Lula’s running mate and would not run otherwise. The Senate seat is also being contested by Marina Silva and Márcio França.

item.title: Haddad rebate críticas e nega sacrifício por ser candidato em SP: ‘Não me conhecem’
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad (PT) dismissed criticisms of his São Paulo gubernatorial candidacy, denying it is a sacrifice and stating he is running to win, not to negotiate. He will leave the Finance Ministry on Friday, to be replaced by Dario Durigan, and begin campaigning in April after the party transfer window closes. Marina Silva and Márcio França are mentioned as potential running mates for vice president and Senate.

item.title: Flávio é “ladrão” e representa “direita corrupta”, diz Renan Santos
item.description: Renan Santos, MBL coordinator and presidential pré-candidate for the Missão party, accused Senator Flávio Bolsonaro of being a 'thief' and representing a 'pro-corruption right', contrasting it with his own vision of a right 'with shame'. He claimed to be more intelligent and honest than other candidates, ruled out supporting another candidate in a runoff, and emphasized his campaign strategy of direct voter contact and youth outreach, where he holds 10% support among Gen Z according to Datafolha.

item.title: Deputada que fez blackface se registrou como parda em 2022
item.description: State deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL-SP) is accused of electoral fraud for declaring herself 'parda' in 2022 to access racial quotas, despite having identified as 'branca' in 2020 and performing a blackface act during an Alesp session in March 2026. Her defense argues the 2022 registration was already approved by TRE-SP and MPE, citing Black and Indigenous ancestry, while federal deputy Erika Hilton (Psol-SP) filed an electoral complaint. The case centers on alleged misuse of racial quotas, freedom of expression, and racial self-identification.

item.title: Moro faz as pazes com o bolsonarismo em ano eleitoral, após duras críticas
item.description: Sérgio Moro left the União Brasil party to join the Liberal Party, which is allied with Jair Bolsonaro, in a move brokered by Senator Rogério Marinho and approved by Flávio and Jair Bolsonaro. The shift occurs in an election year and includes political backing, party infrastructure, and campaign space, following a history of sharp criticism from Moro toward Bolsonarism.

item.title: “Corremos risco de entregar democracia outra vez aos fascistas”, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva warned that it is necessary to field the best candidates in the 2026 elections to prevent democracy from being threatened again by fascist forces, during an event announcing Fernando Haddad as the gubernatorial candidate for São Paulo. He emphasized the gravity of the global political situation and the need for constant mobilization by party leaders and activists, stating that voters act only on election day, while militants fight every day.

item.title: Lula diz que cobrará Conselho de Segurança da ONU sobre guerra no Irã
item.description: Lula criticized the five permanent members of the UN Security Council (US, China, Russia, UK, and France) for promoting wars instead of maintaining peace, noting they spend trillions on arms while neglecting food, education, and refugee aid. He announced his 2026 presidential candidacy with Fernando Haddad as the São Paulo gubernatorial candidate and Geraldo Alckmin as his running mate. Lula also blamed the Bolsonaro administration and former Central Bank president Roberto Campos for the Banco Master scandal, stating the institution was approved in September 2019 and that all irregularities occurred during that period.

item.title: Pichação na UFMG sugere matar Nikolas Ferreira
item.description: Federal deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) was targeted by a graffiti at UFMG's Fafich reading 'may Nikolas be tomorrow's Kirk', referencing the September 2025 assassination of pro-Trump activist Charlie Kirk. Ferreira shared the image on X, criticizing those who believe Brazil's problem is only elections and advocating radical change in education and politics. In 2025, he had supported firing employees who celebrated Kirk's death on social media.

item.title: João Campos anuncia pré-candidatura ao governo com vídeo lembrando o pai
item.description: Recife Mayor João Campos (PSB) announced his pre-candidacy for the Pernambuco gubernatorial race in 2026 through a social media video honoring his late father, former governor Eduardo Campos. On the same day, he outlined his majoritarian ticket, naming Senator Humberto Costa (PT) for Senate re-election and former deputy Marília Arraes (PDT) for a Senate seat, while excluding Silvio Costa Filho (Republicanos), who instead chose to run for re-election as a federal deputy.

item.title: STM nega pedido de Bolsonaro contra ministro que elogiou Lula
item.description: The Superior Military Court denied a request by Jair Bolsonaro’s defense to recuse Minister Joseli Parente Camelo from the case regarding the potential loss of Bolsonaro’s military rank, finding the arguments of bias insufficient. The court’s president, Minister Maria Elizabeth Rocha, ruled that the minister’s past comments praising Lula and discussing the January 8 events were generic and did not indicate prejudice. The case, stemming from Bolsonaro’s STF convictions, could lead to the forfeiture of his rank and that of other high-ranking officers, with implications including halted salaries and possible transfer to civilian prisons.

item.title: Missão vai substituir o bolsonarismo, diz Kataguiri em sua filiação
item.description: Federal deputy Kim Kataguiri stated that his new party, Missão (number 14), aims to replace Bolsonarism on the Brazilian right, criticizing the weakening of the movement led by Jair Bolsonaro and pointing to internal contradictions, especially regarding figures like Flávio Bolsonaro. He emphasized that Missão is an ideological party focused on majoritarian elections, without prioritizing political deals for offices, distinguishing itself from the Centrão. Kataguiri also said he will decide by the end of June whether to run for governor of São Paulo or seek re-election to the Chamber of Deputies in 2026.

item.title: Lula diz que tentará reeleição e deixa caminho livre para Alckmin
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced he will run for re-election in 2026 at age 80, stating that as long as he is alive, the far-right will not return to power in Brazil. Speaking at a PT event in São Bernardo do Campo, Lula praised Vice President Geraldo Alckmin’s loyalty and left open the possibility of Alckmin being his running mate again, without confirming it formally. He also announced Fernando Haddad as the PT’s pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo and emphasized the need to strengthen the ticket with state senators.

item.title: Paes deixa a Prefeitura do Rio com promessa quebrada, linguagem bolsonarista e apoio a Lula
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes (PSD) resigns from office to run for state governor in 2026, breaking his campaign promise to complete his fourth term. While maintaining support for Lula, he adopts Bolsonarist rhetoric by using the term 'neutralize' for criminal deaths in confrontations and aligns with allies of Flávio Bolsonaro, naming Washington Reis as his running mate. His resignation marks the first by a Rio mayor since redemocratization after over 13 years in office.

item.title: Ratinho Jr. e Caiado têm menos mulheres no 1º escalão que Zema e Eduardo Leite
item.description: Governors Romeu Zema (Novo-MG) and Eduardo Leite (PSD-RS) lead in female representation in the first tier among 2026 presidential pre-candidates, with 37% and 34% respectively, while Ratinho Jr. (PSD-PR) and Ronaldo Caiado (PSD-GO) show the lowest proportions at 12% and 14%. Despite progress, none of the governors reach gender parity, as all remain below 40% women in leadership roles. The Folha survey considers only appointed officials equivalent to state secretaries and attorneys general, excluding positions like Fundepar in Paraná.

item.title: Haddad busca vice ligado ao agro para se fortalecer no interior de SP
item.description: Fernando Haddad, chosen by Lula to run for Governor of São Paulo in 2026, is seeking a vice-presidential candidate tied to agribusiness to strengthen his campaign in the state's interior, where he lost to Tarcísio de Freitas in 2022. The strategy aims to emulate Lula's alliance with Geraldo Alckmin, seeking to broaden the PT's electorate with a younger profile close to the agricultural sector, akin to a younger version of Roberto Rodrigues.

item.title: Tarcísio decide manter vice na chapa mesmo sob pressão do PL
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) has decided to keep his running mate, Vice Governor Felício Ramuth (PSD), on the ticket for re-election in 2026, despite pressure from the PL to claim the spot. Allies say the decision is firm, reflecting the existing alliance between the parties. The move comes amid intense negotiations over the majoritarian slate in São Paulo.

item.title: "Ovo da serpente do Bolsonaro", diz Lula sobre caso do Banco Master
item.description: President Lula accused the opposition of trying to blame the PT for the Banco Master scandal, stating it was caused by actions of Jair Bolsonaro and Roberto Campos Neto, former Central Bank president. He spoke at an event in São Bernardo do Campo that formalized Fernando Haddad’s pre-candidacy for São Paulo governor, criticizing current politics as "rotten" and citing the Banco Master case and the "INSS Party" as examples of widespread promiscuity.

item.title: Haddad diz que candidatura não é "sacrifício": "Embate é para ganhar"
item.description: Fernando Haddad rejected the notion that his gubernatorial run in São Paulo is a 'sacrifice', calling it a 'great privilege' to fight alongside the people. Speaking at an event with Lula, Alckmin, and ministers, Haddad stressed that the purpose of electoral combat is to win, not just participate, and that political defeats can be avoided with the right approach.

item.title: Tarcísio se reúne com ministros do STF e reforça pedido de prisão domiciliar para Bolsonaro
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas met with five STF ministers to reinforce the request for house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro, citing the ex-president’s health condition following hospitalization for a lung infection. The meetings also addressed the imminent judgment on the Sabesp privatization law. Bolsonaro, convicted of attempting a coup, is currently hospitalized after being transferred from the Papuda penitentiary.

item.title: El ministro Haddad acepta el encargo de Lula y repite como candidato al Gobierno de São Paulo
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad accepted President Lula's invitation to run for Governor of São Paulo in the 2026 elections, announcing his candidacy at a symbolic event at the metalworkers' union headquarters in São Bernardo do Campo. Haddad stated he is returning to politics after his release from prison, emphasizing the importance of winning in São Paulo — a pivotal state for national elections due to its large population and economic weight.

item.title: Lula confirma Tebet como candidata ao Senado por SP, mas deixa segunda vaga em aberto
item.description: President Lula confirmed Simone Tebet as a Senate candidate for São Paulo in 2026, but left the second seat open, stating that Geraldo Alckmin must decide whether to run. Lula expressed his preference for Alckmin to join the ticket to strengthen it, but emphasized the final decision rests with Alckmin after consulting his wife and Haddad. Marina Silva was also mentioned as a potential candidate.

item.title: ‘Vaga de vice está aberta para ele’, diz Lula sobre Alckmin
item.description: President Lula stated that the vice-presidential spot in 2026 is open for Geraldo Alckmin to decide whether to remain on the ticket or run for the Senate, praising his loyalty and competence. He added that if Alckmin does not pursue the Senate, the PT must build a competitive slate, mentioning Simone Tebet and Marina Silva as potential candidates. Lula also noted that Haddad leads in polls for the São Paulo gubernatorial race, while Alckmin and other ministers are well-positioned for the Senate.

item.title: Tarcísio se encontra com Moraes e pede domiciliar a Bolsonaro
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas met with Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes to request house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro. The meeting was part of a series of talks with five STF ministers, and was the only one in which Bolsonaro's detention was discussed.

item.title: Lula diz que vaga de vice está aberta para Alckmin, mas admite que ele pode concorrer ao Senado
item.description: Lula stated that the vice-presidential spot on his ticket is open for Geraldo Alckmin, saying he would be 'immensely happy' to have him again as his running mate. However, he advised Alckmin to discuss with Fernando Haddad the possibility of running for the Senate in São Paulo, as the ticket needs a strong senatorial candidate. Lula also noted that Simone Tebet will be a Senate candidate in São Paulo and that he removed Guilherme Boulos from the race by appointing him to government.

item.title: Lula diz que vice para Alckmin está "aberta", mas faz apelo por Senado
item.description: Lula stated that the vice-presidential spot on his 2026 ticket is "open" to Geraldo Alckmin, but urged him to consider a Senate run in São Paulo to strengthen the ticket with Fernando Haddad, praising Alckmin’s loyalty and support. He emphasized the need for Alckmin and Haddad to discuss the optimal strategic role for the upcoming elections.

item.title: Haddad anuncia pré-candidatura: “Disputo a eleição para ganhar”
item.description: Fernando Haddad announced his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo in an event alongside President Lula at the Metalworkers' Union in São Bernardo do Campo. The announcement was attended by ministers, lawmakers, and leaders from at least four governing coalition parties. Haddad stated he is running to win the election.

item.title: Lula diz que vaga de vice está aberta para Alckmin, mas não o descarta no Senado
item.description: President Lula stated that the vice-presidential spot on his 2026 re-election ticket is open for Geraldo Alckmin, but the decision to run for executive or Senate will be up to Alckmin. He also announced that Minister Simone Tebet will run for the Senate from São Paulo, switching from MDB to PSB and changing her electoral domicile from Mato Grosso do Sul. Lula criticized former Central Bank President Roberto Campos Neto, linking him to the Banco Master scandal and calling for a thorough investigation.

item.title: Lula diz que Master é 'ovo da serpente' de Bolsonaro e de Roberto Campos Neto, ex-presidente do BC
item.description: President Lula stated that the Banco Master case is the 'spawn of the serpent' of the Bolsonaro government and Roberto Campos Neto's tenure at the Central Bank, claiming the bank was created in 2019 and used for frauds causing R$ 50 billion in losses. He vowed to fully investigate and denied that the PT or his government is responsible, calling it an attempt to shift blame.

item.title: Os riscos de faltar diesel e de paulada nos preços no Brasil
item.description: Brazil faces diesel shortage risks due to constrained global supply and Petrobras' below-market pricing, which discourages private imports. Although stocks may last about 25 days, experts warn that without price adjustments, scarcity could worsen, particularly as the Lula government pushes for state-level subsidies to mitigate electoral fallout.

item.title: Haddad anuncia pré-candidatura ao Governo de SP, nega barganha e diz que entra para ganhar
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT) announced his pre-candidacy for Governor of São Paulo at an event with Lula, denying that his decision is motivated by political bargains and stating he is running to win. He emphasized the need for a clean project to awaken the electorate’s conscience in São Paulo. Lula praised Haddad’s dialogue skills with Congress and urged attendees to use their cell phones to defeat the far right.

item.title: Secretário da Polícia Civil do Rio, Felipe Curi, deixa cargo para concorrer à Câmara
item.description: Rio de Janeiro's Civil Police Secretary, delegate Felipe Curi, announced he will resign to run for a seat in the Chamber of Deputies in the 2026 elections. He argued that policing alone cannot solve crime and stressed the need for federal legislative changes. Curi denied political interference in Operation Red Legacy and affirmed his work remains impartial.

item.title: Lula critica exploração de minerais e defende união da América Latina
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the exploitation of critical minerals and rare earths in Latin America, warning against repeating the historical extraction of the region's natural resources. He stressed the need for Latin American countries to unite to preserve their strategic wealth and avoid dependence on costly imported industrialized goods.

item.title: PT anuncia Haddad como pré-candidato ao governo de São Paulo
item.description: PT president Edinho Silva announced Finance Minister Fernando Haddad as the party's pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo in 2026, during an event in São Bernardo do Campo with Lula and Geraldo Alckmin present. Haddad will resign from the Ministry of Finance, with his departure to be published in the Official Gazette on March 20, 2026. The candidacy is seen as strategic to strengthen Lula's slate and organize the presidential race against Flávio Bolsonaro, given São Paulo's large electorate.

item.title: De volta às urnas contra Tarcísio, Haddad tenta reescrever história em São Paulo
item.description: Fernando Haddad announced his candidacy for governor of São Paulo in October 2026, backed by Lula, aiming to unseat Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos). Although early polls show Tarcísio leading, Haddad insists victory is possible through a clean project and popular mobilization, citing his achievements at the Finance Ministry as the foundation of his campaign.

item.title: Ao lado de Lula, Haddad é confirmado como pré-candidato ao governo de São Paulo
item.description: Fernando Haddad was confirmed as the PT's pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo in an event alongside President Lula, after announcing his departure from the Finance Ministry. Haddad rejected the notion that his candidacy is a 'sacrifice,' calling it a privilege to fight for the party. The PT aims to prevent the opposition from gaining a large advantage in São Paulo, a state where it has never won a gubernatorial election.

item.title: Lula lança pré-candidatura de Fernando Haddad ao governo de São Paulo
item.description: President Lula announced Fernando Haddad's pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo at an event held at the headquarters of the state metalworkers' union in São Bernardo do Campo. Haddad emphasized his goal to win the election with a clean platform and reaffirmed his commitment to national themes of sovereignty and peace, while Lula praised his above-average negotiation skills with Congress and stated he is 'much better than the current governor'. The event featured PT, PCdoB, PV, and PSB leaders, along with allies such as Alckmin and Boulos.

item.title: Defesa de Roberta Luchsinger aponta erro em relatório da PF
item.description: Roberta Luchsinger's defense told the STF that the Federal Police report contains an error in claiming she paid R$641,000 to the owner of a company linked to Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, 'Lulinha'. The defense argues the amount or transaction is incorrect, challenging the validity of the suspicions against the businesswoman and the president's son.

item.title: Haddad confirma pré-candidatura ao governo de São Paulo
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad (PT) confirmed his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo in the 2026 elections, following weeks of internal PT pressure and meetings with President Lula. Haddad stated he will focus on the campaign starting in April, after the party transfer window closes, and will leave the Finance Ministry on Friday, to be replaced by Dario Durigan. Environment Minister Marina Silva and Entrepreneurship Minister Márcio França are being considered for the vice-governor and Senate candidacy, respectively.

item.title: PT anuncia pré-candidatura de Haddad ao governo de SP
item.description: The PT announced Fernando Haddad as its pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo at an event in the Metalworkers' Union of ABC. Despite the announcement, a Datafolha poll shows Governor Tarcísio de Freitas leading with 44% of voting intentions against Haddad's 31% in a first-round scenario. Other candidates such as Kim Kataguiri, Paulo Serra, and Felipe D'Avila perform poorly, with 11% blank/null/none and 1% undecided.

item.title: Comitê do Senado dos EUA aprova novo indicado de Trump para Segurança Interna
item.description: The U.S. Senate Homeland Security Committee voted 8-7 to advance Markwayne Mullin’s nomination to lead the Department of Homeland Security, backed by nearly all Republicans and one Democrat, John Fetterman. Despite the committee approval, committee chair Rand Paul voted against the nomination and threatened to block it, citing concerns over Mullin’s undisclosed international travel while in the House. A final confirmation vote is expected in the Senate floor next week, amid pressure to expedite Trump’s mass deportation agenda.

item.title: Papo Antagonista – Pesquisa desaprova Erika Hilton na Comissão das Mulheres
item.description: A poll mentioned on Papo Antagonista shows disapproval of Deputy Erika Hilton as a potential president of the Women’s Commission in the Chamber of Deputies. It was also reported that Sergio Moro has joined the PL party, Jair Bolsonaro’s party, to run for governor of Paraná in 2026. The program featured Bruno Soller commenting on the poll results.

item.title: Lula destaca importância de terras raras do Brasil para América do Sul
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva defended in São Bernardo do Campo that Brazil's rare earths and critical minerals are key to restoring citizenship in South America, rejecting foreign exploitation as a form of neocolonialism. He likened external attempts to extract these resources to historical actions by the Spanish and English, asserting that only Latin American nations can solve their own sovereignty and development challenges. Lula also honored former Uruguayan president Pepe Mujica, awarding him an honorary doctorate in memoriam from UFABC, praising his humanity and leadership.

item.title: Governo Lula vê sinal positivo em encontro de Trump com líder do Japão
item.description: The Lula administration views the meeting between Donald Trump and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi as a positive signal that the U.S. diplomatic agenda is being unlocked after the Middle East war. This raises expectations that the previously agreed-upon meeting between Lula and Trump, set for January, may soon take place, focusing on cooperation against organized crime, critical minerals, and regional security.

item.title: Guerra do Irã altera eleição brasileira e obriga Lula a se mexer
item.description: The Iran war is driving up oil and gas prices, fueling inflation and threatening Lula’s 2026 re-election bid. The government has responded by eliminating federal diesel taxes and pressuring states to cut ICMS, fearing a trucker strike like in 2018 that boosted Bolsonaro. With crude above $100 per barrel and no signs of the conflict ending soon, the energy crisis has become an unpredictable and potentially decisive factor in Brazil’s election.

item.title: Pressão por domiciliar a Bolsonaro mobiliza Tarcísio, Flávio, Michelle, deputados e ministros do STF
item.description: Allies of Jair Bolsonaro, including Flávio and Michelle Bolsonaro, Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, bolsonarist congressional deputies, and even STF ministers, are pressuring Alexandre de Moraes to grant house arrest to the former president, citing his worsened health following hospitalization for bacterial aspiration pneumonia. The strategy also invokes Moraes’ alleged fear of being implicated in investigations tied to the Banco Master case — involving his wife — and argues that Bolsonaro’s potential death in prison would be politically blamed on the minister.

item.title: Lula é aguardado em sindicato do ABC para lançamento de pré-candidatura de Haddad
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva accompanied Finance Minister Fernando Haddad to the headquarters of the ABC Metalworkers Union in São Bernardo do Campo (SP) for the launch of Haddad’s pre-candidacy for the São Paulo state governorship. The event was widely anticipated as a key moment in the PT’s political strategy for the 2026 elections, underscoring Lula’s strong backing of his ally’s campaign.

item.title: Lula prega união da América do Sul em defesa de terras raras
item.description: President Lula urged greater unity among South American nations in the protection and sustainable exploitation of Brazil's critical minerals and rare earths, during an event honoring former Uruguayan president Pepe Mujica in São Bernardo do Campo. He emphasized that these resources should support the recovery of Latin American citizenship, avoiding a repeat of colonial-era exploitation patterns. This was Lula's second engagement in São Paulo on that day.

item.title: A 1ª pesquisa testando a pré-candidatura de Vanderlei Luxemburgo ao Senado no Tocantins
item.description: The first poll testing Vanderlei Luxemburgo's pre-candidacy for the Senate in Tocantins will be released by Real Time Big Data next Wednesday (25), based on 1,600 interviews with a two-point margin of error. The survey will pit Luxemburgo (Podemos) against Alexandre Guimarães (MDB), Carlos Gaguim (União), Eduardo Gomes (PL), Irajá Abreu (PSD), Wanderley Barbosa and Amélio Cayres (Republicanos), while also updating voter intentions for the state governor race.
